instruction: Leave the closet, and walk out of the bedroom. Stop once you exit the bedroom door.

text: Let me start by taking a closer look at what we're working with.
I'm in a closet facing the shelves and clothes rods, so I need to turn around 180 degrees to find the way out.
I notice a doorway on my left—I should turn toward it to get a better look.
I notice the curtain and light coming through what might be the closet exit, so I'm turning back left to find that door I spotted earlier.

I'm retracing my steps through this space. I turned 180 degrees from the original view and saw a door with a towel rack and curtain, then rotated back toward where I started. Now I need to turn left 90 degrees to get back to that door view.

I'm looking at this scene from the curtain view again—there's a doorway with light coming through behind the curtain on the right, but I want to turn left to get a better look at the door with the towel.
I notice the closet door on the left with the towel rack and decide to head toward it.
I can see an archway leading out of the closet into another room with a mirror visible—that's the exit I need to go through.
I'm moving through the archway and can now see into the bedroom with the bed visible on the right side, confirming I'm exiting the closet.
I've bumped into the door itself—I'm looking at the wooden surface up close. Let me reorient and find the archway to move through.
Now I can see through the archway into the bedroom with a bed visible. This is the exit from the closet into the bedroom. Let me proceed forward through the archway into the bedroom.

I'm in the bedroom now and can see the bed and the space around me. I notice an archway in the center background that might lead somewhere, and there's a decorative iron gate on the right side. Let me look around more carefully to find the exit door.
I'm looking at a close-up of heavy curtains with an office space visible to the right, which could be another area to explore. Let me shift my perspective to get a better sense of what's around me.
I'm back in the original bedroom view and need to explore the right side to locate the door, so I'm turning further to the right.
That's just a decorative curtain by the iron screen, not the way out. I should head right instead.
This appears to be a different section of the closet rather than the bedroom exit, so I'm turning right to continue exploring the space.

I've completed a full 360-degree rotation and returned to the curtain view, confirming the room layout includes the bed area, iron screen with curtain, a closet space, and the desk area visible through the gap. The instruction is prompting me to walk out of the room.

Since I need to find the actual bedroom door rather than just exploring closets, I should head toward that desk area visible through the curtain gap—it appears to be a different room that might lead to the exit.
I'm too close to the obstacle, so I need to back up and try a different approach. Let me turn right to see if I can get around the edge of the curtain.
I've moved into a hallway or alcove and can now see the bedroom from a different angle. I should look for a way out by turning right to explore what's in that direction.

I notice there's a black rectangle artifact in the top right corner, but I'm looking at the same bed view. There's an archway visible in the center-back of the room beyond the bed that might lead further into the hallway, so I'm moving toward it.

I'm noticing the layout more clearly now—there's a hallway branching off to the right past the bed with an archway, and windows along the left side. This appears to be a spacious bedroom, so I should head toward that arched corridor in the middle-right to see what's beyond.

I'm noticing a corridor with arched passages that seems to lead toward a bathroom or adjacent room, and there's a partially open dark wooden door on the right that might be an exit from the bedroom—I should move closer to investigate it.

I notice there's an archway leading to a hallway with tiled flooring that seems to branch off in a different direction—possibly toward a bathroom or exit. I should explore this path instead of the closet route.

I'm looking at what appears to be a hallway with a partially open dark wooden door on the right and a corridor extending to the left—this might be the bedroom's main exit. I should move forward to see what's ahead.
I notice an open doorway to the right leading into a hallway with a tiled floor—this must be the bedroom exit. I'll head through it.

I can see an open door on the right leading into a hallway with tiled flooring that stretches toward another room—this must be the bedroom exit. I'm moving through it now, stepping out into the hallway.
I'm moving through the doorway now, with the wooden door panel right beside me, and ahead there's a tiled corridor opening into a room with dark furniture—looks like I'm leaving the bedroom behind.

I've moved through the doorway into the hallway now, though there's still a wall edge visible on the right from the door frame. Let me push forward a bit more to ensure I'm completely clear of the bedroom and fully in the corridor.

I've exited the bedroom and entered a hallway with tiled flooring and a curved archway—this matches the goal of leaving through the bedroom door. I'm a couple steps past the threshold and should still be within three meters of the exit point, so I'll stop here.
I have exited through the bedroom door and am now in the hallway/corridor outside the bedroom, with the door behind me. This matches the instruction's endpoint of exiting the bedroom door.

I have completed the navigation task. Starting from inside the closet (facing shelving/clothing rods), I turned to locate the closet's exit door, passed through an archway into the bedroom (identified by the bed, ceiling beams, and furnishings), then traversed across the bedroom to find its exit door on the far side. I proceeded through that wooden door into the adjoining tiled hallway, confirming I had exited the bedroom, and issued the STOP action there.
